---
title: Upload (sda-cli)
---

This is the core of the direct path: encrypt your prepared dataset and upload it
to your BigPicture inbox with `sda-cli`. Work through the steps in order.

::: {.callout-note}
Make sure `sda-cli` is installed first — see
[Prerequisites (alternative path)](prerequisites.qmd) — and that your dataset is
laid out and validated per [Prepare data](prepare-data.qmd) and
[Validate metadata](validate-metadata.qmd).
:::

## 0. Test submission (recommended)

We strongly recommend a small test upload to the **staging** environment first,
so you don't have to re-upload terabytes if something is wrong. Upload two
images with metadata generated per [Prepare data](prepare-data.qmd) — include
all XML files, the `PRIVATE` folder (always required), and the `LANDING_PAGE`
folder (if you want a landing page).

Follow the same steps below, but log in at the
[staging service](https://staging-login.bp.nbis.se/) instead of production.

## 1. Sign and send the Terms of Use form

Sign the
[Terms of Use form](https://bigpictureeu.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/wp1/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B5914340E-2976-4454-BD7A-1DE64825EDC5%7D&file=BigPicture%20ToU%20v2.6.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true)
*(internal)* and submit it to [submit@bigpicture.eu](mailto:submit@bigpicture.eu).

## 2. Download the encryption key

For BigPicture to read your files, they must be encrypted with the correct
**public key**. Download it:

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows
```powershell
curl -OL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NBISweden/EGA-SE-user-docs/main/crypt4gh_bp_key.pub
```
## macOS
```bash
curl -OL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NBISweden/EGA-SE-user-docs/main/crypt4gh_bp_key.pub
```
## Linux
```bash
curl -OL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NBISweden/EGA-SE-user-docs/main/crypt4gh_bp_key.pub
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
ls crypt4gh_bp_key.pub
```

The file should be present in your working directory.
:::

## 3. Encrypt the files

With the public key in place, encrypt your submission files. `sda-cli` creates
its own encryption key automatically.

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows
```powershell
sda-cli encrypt --key crypt4gh_bp_key.pub <file_1_to_encrypt> <file_2_to_encrypt> ...
```
## macOS
```bash
./sda-cli encrypt --key crypt4gh_bp_key.pub <file_1_to_encrypt> <file_2_to_encrypt> ...
```
## Linux
```bash
./sda-cli encrypt --key crypt4gh_bp_key.pub <file_1_to_encrypt> <file_2_to_encrypt> ...
```
:::

The tool also writes four checksum files — **save these, you need them during
submission**:

```
checksum_encrypted.md5
checksum_encrypted.sha256
checksum_unencrypted.md5
checksum_unencrypted.sha256
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

Each input file should now have an encrypted `.c4gh` counterpart, and the four
checksum files should exist:

```bash
ls *.c4gh checksum_*.md5 checksum_*.sha256
```
:::

## 4. Download your configuration file and upload

1.  Log in at the [BigPicture login portal](https://login.bp.nbis.se/) and
    follow the prompts to authenticate. Click **Download credentials to upload
    to the Inbox** to get your `s3cmd-inbox.conf` file. *(For a test upload, use
    the [staging service](https://staging-login.bp.nbis.se/) instead.)*

2.  Upload your encrypted files. By default the local folder structure is **not**
    preserved — all files land in the root of your inbox. Use
    `--target-directory <path>` to place them in a specific remote folder.

    To upload files individually:

    ::: {.panel-tabset}
    ## Windows
    ```powershell
    sda-cli --config <configuration_file> upload <encrypted_file_1> <encrypted_file_2> ...
    ```
    ## macOS
    ```bash
    ./sda-cli --config <configuration_file> upload <encrypted_file_1> <encrypted_file_2> ...
    ```
    ## Linux
    ```bash
    ./sda-cli --config <configuration_file> upload <encrypted_file_1> <encrypted_file_2> ...
    ```
    :::

    To upload an entire directory — often more efficient — use the recursive
    `-r` flag, which **preserves** your local folder structure on the remote
    server:

    ::: {.panel-tabset}
    ## Windows
    ```powershell
    sda-cli --config <configuration_file> upload -r <folder_to_upload>
    ```
    ## macOS
    ```bash
    ./sda-cli --config <configuration_file> upload -r <folder_to_upload>
    ```
    ## Linux
    ```bash
    ./sda-cli --config <configuration_file> upload -r <folder_to_upload>
    ```
    :::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

`sda-cli` reports each file as it finishes uploading. For the full set of
commands and flags — which may change between versions — consult the tool's
built-in help, the most reliable reference:

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows
```powershell
sda-cli help
```
## macOS
```bash
./sda-cli help
```
## Linux
```bash
./sda-cli help
```
:::
:::

## 5. Notify us

When the upload is complete, [email the helpdesk](mailto:{{< var email.helpdesk >}})
so we know to process it. Use this subject line:

```
submission - DATASET_{IDENTIFIER}
```

And include in the body:

- the **name of the uploader**, and
- the **name of the dataset**.
